title: 10 best herbal teas for headaches
intro_desc: Herbal teas for headaches are caffeine-free, warm beverages made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices. They are a natural and effective way to alleviate headaches, as they contain soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. Some popular herbal teas for headaches include chamomile, ginger, feverfew, and willow bark. Chamomile tea, for instance, helps relax muscles and reduce stress, which can trigger headaches. Ginger tea, on the other hand, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease headache pain. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can find relief from headaches and improve their overall quality of life. For example, a stressed-out student who frequently suffers from tension headaches can drink chamomile tea before bed to relax and reduce the likelihood of headaches. Similarly, a construction worker who experiences migraines due to inflammation can consume ginger tea during breaks to alleviate pain and continue with their workday comfortably.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cohosh teas helps with headaches because it contains a unique blend of compounds that have natural pain-relieving properties. The tea's active ingredients, including flavonoids and saponins, work together to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles, which can help alleviate headache symptoms such as tension and migraine pain. Additionally, black cohosh has been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, helping to restore balance and reduce discomfort associated with headaches.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Black cohosh's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches, such as migraines and tension headaches.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in black cohosh tea can help relax blood vessels and muscles, which can help alleviate spasms and constrictions that can lead to headache pain.

Antioxidant properties: Black cohosh's antioxidant compounds can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, inflammation, and cell damage, all of which can contribute to the development of headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in triterpene glycosides, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relieve headache pain.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain a high concentration of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried black cohosh roots or use pre-dried commercial products in amounts of one to two teaspoons per cup.
Boil fresh water in a tea kettle for five to seven minutes to reach a temperature between 195°f and 205°f.
Combine the dried black cohosh root with the boiling water in a teapot or infuser and let it steep for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot and discard the solids immediately after straining.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving hot or storing it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to two days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo teas helps with headaches because it contains a natural analgesic property that helps to soothe and relax tense muscles, alleviating pain and discomfort. The tea's active ingredients, such as coumarins and alkaloids, work together to calm the nervous system, reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the brain. This can help to ease tension headaches, migraines, and other types of headaches, providing fast and effective relief from painful symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yanhusuo tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the blood vessels, which can contribute to headaches.

Pain-relieving property: The bioactive compounds in yanhusuo tea have analgesic and anti-pain properties that can help to relieve headache pain by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain.
Vasodilatory property: Yanhusuo tea contains substances that can help to dilate blood vessels, which can improve blood flow to the head and brain, reducing the frequency and severity of headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, relieving tension and stress that can contribute to headaches.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have a warming effect when used in teas, which helps to relax muscle tension and improve blood circulation, reducing headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh corydalis yanhusuo roots with clean scissors cutting off.
Rinse the harvested roots thoroughly under running cold water for removal of dirt.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces and soak them in pure water for 30 minutes steeping.
Strain the soaked root mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth for liquid extraction retaining.
Combine the extracted tea liquid with boiling hot water at a ratio of 1:8 and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew teas helps with headaches because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The presence of parthenolide, a key compound in feverfew, blocks the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause blood vessels to dilate and lead to painful headaches. Additionally, feverfew's flavonoids help to stabilize mast cells, reducing the release of histamine, another culprit behind migraine pain. By addressing these underlying causes, feverfew tea provides a natural and effective way to alleviate headache symptoms and promote relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew tea contains parthenolide, a compound that reduces inflammation and swelling in blood vessels, which can contribute to headache relief by relaxing tense muscles and improving circulation.

Pain-relieving flavonoids: Feverfew tea is rich in flavonoids like luteolin and apigenin, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pain-promoting chemicals in the body, helping to reduce headache severity and duration.

Vasoconstrictive properties: The parthenolide in feverfew tea can also cause blood vessels to constrict, which helps to relieve tension headaches by reducing blood flow to the brain and alleviating feelings of pressure and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots are valued for their ability to ease pain and reduce inflammation, making them an effective component in herbal teas for headache relief.
Flowers: Feverfew flowers contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried feverfew flowers and one teaspoon of lemon balm leaves.
Combine the measured herbs with one cup of boiling water in a large heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes, allowing the flavors and properties to infuse.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming or refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with headaches because its unique combination of flavonoids, antioxidants, and essential oils work together to relax and calm the nervous system. The tea's calming properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for headaches. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in maidenhair tree tea may also help to constrict blood vessels and reduce pain associated with migraines and tension headaches. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, maidenhair tree teas provide a natural and effective way to alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba tea's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to headache development.

Vasodilatory Effects: The bioactive compounds in Ginkgo biloba tea, such as terpenoids and flavonoids, have been shown to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing blood pressure, which can help alleviate headaches caused by vascular constriction.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Ginkgo biloba tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and pain in the head and neck, which are common symptoms of tension-type headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree are used due to their ability to calm and relax the mind and body, providing relief from headache symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have a soothing effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce tension and ease pain associated with headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves or purchase dried ones in adequate quantities.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves per cup.
Combine the measured leaves with boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or cups to remove solids.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable drinking temperature before serving hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with headaches because its active compound, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in blood vessels. This, in turn, can ease tension and pressure on the brain, which may be contributing to the headache. Additionally, licorice tea's flavonoids and isoflavones have been shown to have analgesic and sedative effects, helping to relax the mind and body, and ultimately providing relief from headache symptoms.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches such as migraines and tension headaches.

Flavonoids: Licorice tea contains flavonoids like licoricidin and glyasperin, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate headache symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Glycyrrhetinic acid: This compound has a synergistic effect with other herbs and can help relax blood vessels, reduce blood pressure, and calm the nervous system, which may help relieve tension headaches and migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to make tea as they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps to relieve headache pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems, are used to create tea as they possess anti-anxiety and calming properties that can help alleviate headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots weighing 2-3 tablespoons for every cup of water.
Rinse the roots with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris and pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Chop the roots into small pieces and place them in a teapot or infuser containing one quart of boiling water.
Steep the tea mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the desired strength is achieved by taste or aroma.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or carafe and serve hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel teas helps with headaches because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The tea's active compounds, such as salicylic acid and gallic acid, work together to reduce swelling in blood vessels, ease tension, and relax muscle contractions that can contribute to headache pain. As the tea calms and soothes the body, it also helps to clear any blockages in the sinuses, further alleviating pressure and discomfort associated with headaches.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Witch hazel contains high levels of tannins, which are compounds that can help reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels, providing relief from tension headaches by decreasing swelling and pressure in the brain.

Salicylic acid: Salicylic acid is a natural pain reliever found in witch hazel that can help alleviate headache symptoms by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause pain and inflammation.

Antioxidants: Witch hazel contains antioxidants like polyphenols and flavonoids, which can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, potentially reducing the frequency and severity of migraines and other types of headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the witch hazel plant contain a high concentration of tannins, which can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, providing relief from headache pain.
Bark: Witch hazel bark has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and headaches due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves and twigs from mature plants early in the morning.
Rinse the harvested material with cool water to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Chop the cleaned witch hazel into small pieces using scissors or a herb cutter carefully.
Steep 2 tablespoons of chopped witch hazel in boiling water for 5-7 minutes patiently.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup quickly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort teas helps with headaches because its active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect. When consumed as a tea, these compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream, where they can help to relax tense muscles and reduce blood vessel constriction, both of which are common contributors to headache pain. This relaxation response can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often linked to tension headaches.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: A natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce pain and swelling in the head, making it effective in alleviating headache symptoms.

Hypotensive effects: St. John's Wort tea has been shown to help lower blood pressure, which can be a contributing factor to headaches such as migraines and tension headaches, by reducing blood vessel constriction.

Adaptogenic properties: St. John's Wort tea is known for its ability to balance the body's response to stress, which can be a common trigger for headaches such as tension headaches; this adaptogenic effect helps to reduce overall stress levels and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in active compounds like hyperforin, which can help alleviate headache symptoms by reducing inflammation and anxiety.
Leaves: Contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can provide relief from headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried st john's wort flowers and leaves from a trusted source ensuring correct identity.
Rinse 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb or 2-3 tablespoons of fresh herb with filtered water to remove impurities.
Steep the herbal material in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its bioactive compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to separate the solids from the liquid.
Enjoy the warm or cooled st john's wort tea within 24 hours of preparation, taking 1/2 cup up to three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with headaches because of its calming and relaxing properties. The tea's potent essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, work to soothe the mind and body, reducing tension and stress that often contribute to headache development. As the tea takes effect, it eases muscle contractions and improves blood flow, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lavender tea's antispasmodic properties help to relax tense muscles and alleviate tension-related headaches by reducing spasms and cramps in blood vessels.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce swelling and pain associated with headaches, including migraines and tension headaches.

Calming and sedative effects: The calming and sedative properties of lavender tea can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for headaches, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, lavender roots have a calming effect on the mind and body, making them effective in soothing headache pain.
Seeds: Containing a unique blend of terpenes and sesquiterpenes, lavender seeds have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavender flowers in the morning for optimal potency and flavor.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers or two tablespoons of fresh lavender buds for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the measured lavender with 8 ounces of boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on personal taste preferences and desired strength of flavor.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, then serve hot or allow it to cool to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort teas helps with headaches because it contains a unique blend of flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that work together to relax tense muscles, calm irritated nerves, and reduce inflammation. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to ease tension headaches by reducing swelling in the blood vessels and promoting blood flow to the brain, while its calming effects soothe the mind and body, providing relief from the throbbing pain of migraines.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory properties: Motherwort tea helps to dilate blood vessels, which can reduce tension and alleviate pain associated with headaches caused by vascular constriction.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Motherwort's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the head and neck that can contribute to headache symptoms.

Calming and sedative effects: Motherwort tea has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system, which can help alleviate tension and anxiety-related headaches by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and relieve tension associated with headaches.
Bark: The bark contains flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate headache symptoms by constricting blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried motherwort leaves and flowers.
Measure one cup of boiling water and pour it over the herbs.
Allow the mixture to steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Enjoy the warm, slightly bitter tea immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Flax teas helps with headaches because of its unique combination of omega-3 fatty acids, antioxidants, and anti-inflammatory properties. The omega-3s in flax help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a contributing factor to headache pain. The antioxidants also work to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and tension headaches. Additionally, flax teas' calming effects on the nervous system can help to soothe and relax the mind and body, providing relief from migraine and tension headaches.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Flax tea contains omega-3 fatty acids, particularly alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), which has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce headache pain and inflammation.
Antioxidant properties: Flax tea is rich in antioxidants, including lignans and polyphenols, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress that may contribute to headaches.

Neuroprotective properties: Flax tea contains a compound called mucilage, which has been shown to have neuroprotective effects by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, potentially helping to alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the flax plant have been traditionally used to make teas for headache relief due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Seeds: Flax seeds are a natural source of omega-3 fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flax flowers and leaves from mature plants weighing approximately one ounce.
Rinse the harvested flax material with clean water to remove any debris or dirt particles.
Combine one teaspoon of dried flax seeds or two teaspoons of fresh flax leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the flax's mucilage and saponins to release into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding solids and serving hot or cooled as desired.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat headaches with herbal teas is by choosing teas with proven therapeutic properties. Peppermint tea, for instance, helps relax constricted blood vessels, while ginger tea is known for its anti-inflammatory effects that can alleviate headache pain. Feverfew tea is another excellent choice, as it contains parthenolide, a compound that inhibits the expansion of blood vessels in the brain, thereby reducing headache symptoms. For best results, drink these teas regularly and always opt for organic, high-quality teas. However, if headaches persist, consult a healthcare professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to headaches that are treated with herbal teas include migraines, tension headaches, and stress-related headaches. Certain herbal teas, such as chamomile, feverfew, and ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the severity and frequency of these headaches. Peppermint tea can also help relieve tension headaches by relaxing the muscles in the head and neck. Additionally, passionflower tea has been shown to help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can prevent headaches caused by stress. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas to treat any medical condition.
